_note: Worked example: Cedar Summit Health Partners, a fictional 12-clinic ambulatory healthcare network. Practice management (ClearSlate PM) owns patients/coverage/appointments, the EHR (MeadowPoint) owns encounters/diagnoses/procedures (derived from completed appointments - real lineage), and the RCM platform (BrightBill) owns charges/claims/remittances. Two state machines run on scheduling.appointment: the scheduling lifecycle (scheduled -> checked_in -> completed / no_show / cancelled) and the visit billing lifecycle anchored on completed_at (claims with denial/resubmission). No real PII: surrogate MRNs, persona-pool names, birth_year only, 555-01xx phones, example.com emails, NPIs in the provably-fictional 9xxxxxxxxx range.

calendar._note: Ambulatory weekday shape: Monday peak, Friday taper, urgent-care-only weekends. Month weights carry the winter respiratory bump (Dec-Feb) and the mid-summer elective dip with a Nov-Dec deductible-met lift.

vocab._note: Per-category service and diagnosis pools so descriptions, fees, and chronic flags stay mutually coherent (category is drawn first, everything else conditions on it).
preventive_dx: Screening for malignant neoplasm of colon

tables.name: application
tables.purpose: Application catalog for the fictional landscape.

tables._note: 12 clinics; the flagship multispecialty hub carries an outsized share of the panel via zipf clinic assignment on patients.
tables.name: clinic
tables.purpose: Clinic site master for the ambulatory network.

tables._note: Payer master with per-payer contracted rates. 'Self-Pay / Patient Direct' is a configured financial class like real PM systems use, so self-pay visits flow through the same claim machinery with zero payer payment.
tables.name: payer
tables.purpose: Payer / financial-class master with contracted reimbursement rates.

tables._note: Chargemaster: category drawn first, then description and fee both condition on it. Fees are whole-dollar (price_endings [0.0]) like a real fee schedule, which also gives downstream amounts honest duplicate mass.
tables.name: fee_schedule
tables.purpose: Chargemaster / fee schedule for billable services.

tables._note: Diagnosis reference: ICD-10-style code prefix conditions on the clinical category (J respiratory, E metabolic, I cardio, M msk, F mental health, Z preventive, S injury), as does the chronic flag.
tables.name: diagnosis_code
tables.purpose: Diagnosis code reference used by encounter coding.

tables._note: Providers carry the npi identifier kind (9xxxxxxxxx range can never be a real NPI but keeps the 80840 Luhn check digit). Clinic assignment is zipf so the hub clinic staffs the most providers, matching its patient share.
tables.name: provider
tables.purpose: Rendering providers (physicians and APPs) across the network.

tables._note: Patient master. NO real PII: surrogate MRN, persona-pool names, birth_year only (no DOB), reserved-range phone, example.com email. created_at is sorted with 70% backfill so MRN sequence correlates with registration date and the panel is tenured rather than ramping from zero. utilization_tier + a heavy-tailed per-patient multiplier on appointments produce the chronic/complex whale cohort.
tables.name: patient
tables.purpose: Practice management patient master (registration system of record).

columns.name: home_clinic_id
columns._note: zipf: the flagship hub holds roughly a third of the panel, satellites tail off.

columns.name: created_at
columns._note: sorted + backfill: registrations predate the data window for 85% of the panel, so in-window encounter volume grows at a believable ~20-30%/yr (panel growth + 5% calendar trend) instead of doubling.

tables._note: Coverage: rank 1 is the billed primary (payer mix ~ Medicare .37 / Medicaid .16 / commercial .37 / self-pay .06), rank 2+ are supplements/secondaries. payer_id uses fk affinity matching on payer_type with a 2% leak (mis-mapped plans at registration). term_date mostly null/future; the stale_mapping imperfection retro-terms 4% to feed eligibility-denial DQ-005.
tables.name: coverage
tables.purpose: Insurance coverage on file per patient.

columns.name: plan_code
columns._note: The payer's plan/product code as keyed at registration. The revenue-cycle team groups these into financial classes via manual.payer_plan_grouping. ~14 distinct codes (payer plus a product family); one live code (HBR-EPO) is left UNMAPPED by the mapping table, two map to retired financial classes.

tables._note: THE central table: every appointment, kept or not. Two machines run here. Machine 1 (scheduling) anchors on appointment_date: ~12% no-show, ~12% cancel, 1.5% never reconciled (stale 'scheduled' backlog -> DQ-010). Machine 2 (visit billing / claim lifecycle) anchors on completed_at, which machine 1 writes first - rows that never completed are skipped and legitimately stay 'not_billed'. Volume is segment-conditioned (complex patients ~12x low utilizers) with a heavy-tailed per-patient multiplier.
tables.name: appointment
tables.purpose: Appointments across all clinics, including no-shows and cancellations.

columns.name: clinic_id
columns._note: Affinity match on the patient's home clinic with a 6% leak (urgent-care walk-ins, coverage at sister sites).

columns.name: provider_id
columns._note: Provider drawn from the visit clinic's own staff with a 5% float-pool leak.

columns.name: appointment_date
columns._note: Calendar-weighted (Monday peak, near-zero weekends, winter bump) and never before the patient registered.

tables._note: Services booked WITH the appointment (schedulers book specific services), so rows legitimately exist for visits that later no-show or cancel. The fee snapshot is fk_copy'd from the chargemaster and booked_amount = fee x integer units - the required price-realism pattern. Documented procedures derive from these for completed visits only.
tables.name: appointment_service
tables.purpose: Service lines booked on each appointment, priced from the fee schedule.
tables.grain: one row per appointment per booked service line

columns.name: fee_item_id
columns._note: zipf(0.5): popular services (level-3/4 E/M, flu shots, lipid panels) lead without one code dominating.

tables.name: appointment_event
tables.purpose: Scheduling status history emitted by the appointment lifecycle machine.
tables.grain: one row per appointment per scheduling state entered

tables.name: visit_billing_event
tables.purpose: Visit billing / claim lifecycle history emitted by the claim machine.
tables.grain: one row per completed visit per billing state entered

tables.name: job_run
tables.purpose: Interface engine batch run log; each run belongs to a catalog job, with occasional failures.

columns.name: job_id
columns._note: uniform (not zipf): runs spread evenly across the job catalog, no skew check.

columns.name: error_note
columns._note: Non-null only when the run failed or ran partially.

tables.name: rule
tables.purpose: Data quality rule catalog (live results come from the dq_rule_result_current view).

cases.1: completed encounter with no posted charges
cases.5: claim service date after coverage termination
cases.6: staging last name disagrees with PM source
cases.8: charge posted more than 5 days after service
cases.10: past appointment never reconciled from scheduled
cases.11: billable plan code missing approved financial-class mapping
cases.13: re-sent 837 claim file superseded by later batch

tables.name: control_catalog
tables.purpose: Reconciliation control catalog (break rows come from the control_recon_* views).

cases.1: Claim billed ties to posted charges
cases.3: EMPI patients tie to source mappings
cases.4: Revenue-cycle billable encounters reconcile to clinical-ops completed encounters

tables._note: Everything below derives via SQL from the tables above - real lineage, not parallel-faked data.
tables.name: pm_patient_extract
tables.purpose: Nightly PM patient extract as landed: all text, US-format dates.

tables.name: pm_patient
tables.purpose: Typed/cleaned PM patients; normalizes raw extract dates.

tables.name: patient_identifier
tables.purpose: EMPI identifier crosswalk: PM MRN and EHR person id per canonical patient.

tables.name: patient
tables.purpose: Canonical (EMPI golden) patient joining staging and the crosswalk.

tables._note: Encounters exist only for appointments that actually checked in - coherence by construction, never a random FK.
tables.name: encounter
tables.purpose: EHR encounters derived 1:1 from checked-in appointments.

tables.name: diagnosis
tables.purpose: Encounter diagnoses: every encounter has a principal dx, 60% a second, 25% a third; code popularity is banded so chronic heavy-hitters dominate.
tables.grain: one row per encounter per diagnosis rank

tables.name: procedure
tables.purpose: Documented procedures: the booked services of visits that completed.
tables.grain: one row per documented service on a completed encounter

tables.name: charge
tables.purpose: Charge lines posted from documented procedures; 'pending' rows are captured but not yet posted (DNFB).
tables.grain: one row per charge line (restatement pairs append)

tables.name: claim
tables.purpose: Professional claims generated from submitted visits; status mirrors the visit billing machine, payer comes from the patient's primary coverage.
tables.grain: one row per claim (one claim per billed encounter)

tables.name: claim_line
tables.purpose: Claim lines snapshotted from posted charges at claim generation (later charge corrections create recon breaks).
tables.grain: one row per claim per line

tables.name: resubmission
tables.purpose: Corrected resubmissions of denied claims with outcome.

tables._note: Remittances derive from PAID claims. The disposition identity holds exactly by construction: paid + contractual adjustment + patient responsibility = billed. Self-pay 'remits' carry zero payer payment with the balance as patient responsibility after the uninsured discount.
tables.name: remittance
tables.purpose: 835-style remittances for paid claims with full payment disposition.
tables.grain: one row per remittance (one per paid claim)

tables.name: denial_worklist
tables.purpose: Open denial follow-up queue plus mapping-request cases, routed by root-cause reason / case type; carries the registration override flag for root-cause analysis.
tables.grain: one row per open denied claim or per unmapped billable plan code

tables.name: dim_date
tables.purpose: Calendar dimension.

tables.name: dim_patient
tables.purpose: Patient dimension from the canonical EMPI layer.

tables.name: dim_provider
tables.purpose: Provider dimension.

tables.name: fact_encounter
tables.purpose: Encounter fact with payer attribution and gross charges.

tables.name: fact_charge
tables.purpose: Charge-line fact (warehouse refresh snapshot; intentionally lags post-load charge corrections, which the recon view surfaces).
tables.grain: one row per charge line as of the warehouse refresh

tables.name: fact_claim
tables.purpose: Claim fact with adjudication outcome and payment disposition.
tables.grain: one row per claim (latest adjudication snapshot)

tables._note: Landing layer (rung 1). One raw.* table per source feed, beyond the PM patient extract above: the EHR encounter extract, the BrightBill charge interface, the clearinghouse 837 professional-claim file, and the 835 remittance file. Verbatim text payloads, US-format dates, file/batch ids, ingested_at. format_drift corrupts the date columns post-derivation; the DQ views read these live and catch unparseable dates and superseded re-sends.
tables.name: ehr_encounter_extract
tables.purpose: Nightly EHR encounter extract as landed: all text, US-format dates.

tables.name: rcm_charge_extract
tables.purpose: BrightBill charge-interface extract as landed: all text, US-format dates.

tables._note: 837 professional-claim file from the clearinghouse. Re-sends are real: when a batch rejects, the whole file is resubmitted under a new batch_id, so the same claim_id lands twice. landing_id is a single-column surrogate PK (the same claim_id can appear under two batch_ids) so format_drift can target individual landed rows. The stg layer dedups to the latest batch via row_number; DQ-013 reports the superseded duplicates.
tables.name: clearinghouse_837_extract
tables.purpose: Clearinghouse 837 professional-claim file as landed: all text, US-format dates, re-sent batches.

tables.name: remit_835_extract
tables.purpose: 835 remittance file as landed: all text, US-format dates.

tables._note: Staging layer (rung 2). One stg.* per landing table: typed, trimmed, case-guarded date parse (drifted formats land NULL), row_number dedup against re-sent batches.
tables.name: ehr_encounter
tables.purpose: Cleaned EHR encounters; parses landed dates.

tables.name: rcm_charge
tables.purpose: Cleaned charge lines; parses landed dates.

tables.name: claim_837
tables.purpose: Cleaned 837 claims; parses landed dates and dedups re-sent batches to the latest.

tables.name: remit_835
tables.purpose: Cleaned 835 remittances; parses landed dates.

tables._note: Human-entered mapping table. The revenue-cycle team maintains payer plan code -> payer group / financial class in a spreadsheet, uploaded here. Applied asymmetrically: mart_revcycle_* joins it (financial-class grouping), mart_clinicalops_* uses the raw plan code. 12 of 13 live plan codes mapped; HBR-EPO has ONLY an UNAPPROVED row (approved_flag=0) so it stays UNMAPPED. Two retired-plan rows for codes no longer billed (LIB-HMO, TRI-CHOICE, effective_end_date past). One conflicting MCR-ADV-PPO re-upload: two APPROVED active rows, different mapped_code, the later uploaded_at wins via row_number. TOTAL-PRESERVING: every billable encounter counts once, mapped or UNMAPPED, so the recon's unexplained_difference is exactly 0.
tables.name: payer_plan_grouping
tables.purpose: Revenue-cycle-maintained payer plan code to financial-class mapping (spreadsheet upload).

columns.name: approved_flag
columns._note: mapping_id 14 (HBR-EPO) is the single unapproved draft row -> HBR-EPO stays UNMAPPED in mart_revcycle.

tables._note: Job catalog for the code objects below. job_run history references these by job_id; the procedures/function/extracts in catalog.code_object name them. uniform fk from job_run (not zipf) so no skew check fires.
tables.name: job
tables.purpose: Scheduled job catalog (ETL feeds, refresh procedures, outbound extracts).

tables._note: Materialized view (rung 8). SQLite has no native matviews -> a mart table refreshed by an insert-select from bv_ar_by_payer. scale_exempt: ~24 payer-months regardless of multiplier, bounded. Paired with the refresh_mv_month_end_ar_snapshot code object and its job runs.
tables.name: month_end_ar_snapshot
tables.purpose: Month-grain AR snapshot by payer, materialized from bv_ar_by_payer.

tables._note: Code-object catalog (rung: catalog). Self-registers every view from sqlite_master, plus authored target-platform source for the nightly claim-scrubbing procedure, month-end charge-close procedure, the mv refresh procedure, the age_at_service function, and the two outbound extracts (837 submission feed, state immunization registry).
tables.name: code_object
tables.purpose: Catalog of deployed code objects (views, procedures, functions, extracts) with source text.

tables.name: lineage_edge
tables.purpose: Lineage edges linking code objects to the tables they read and write.

state_machines._note: Machine order matters: the scheduling machine writes completed_at, which the claim machine (next) anchors on. Residual 1.5% of past appointments never leave 'scheduled' - the front-desk reconciliation backlog that DQ-010 works.
state_machines.name: appointment_lifecycle

state_machines._note: Claim lifecycle on the same table, anchored on completed_at: rows that never completed are skipped and stay 'not_billed'. Denial rate ~11% of adjudicated; denied claims resubmit (45%), appeal (20%), or write off (28%), with residual open denials feeding the worklist. Right-censoring leaves recent claims mid-adjudication (open AR).
state_machines.name: claim_lifecycle

derivations.name: raw_pm_patient_extract
derivations._note: Raw landing copy with US-format dates; the post-derivation format_drift imperfection corrupts a slice, which DQ-004 catches.
sql: select cast(patient_id as text), mrn, first_name, last_name, sex, cast(birth_year as text),

derivations.name: stg_pm_patient
sql: select cast(patient_id as integer), mrn, trim(first_name), trim(last_name), upper(sex),

derivations.name: xref_patient_identifier
derivations._note: A crosswalk that maps a single system is not a crosswalk - both PM and EHR identifiers are mapped; missing_xref later deletes ~2% to feed DQ-003.
sql: select row_number() over (order by patient_id), 'patient', patient_id, 'ClearSlate PM',
select 400000 + row_number() over (order by patient_id), 'patient', patient_id, 'MeadowPoint EHR',

derivations.name: core_patient
sql: select s.patient_id, s.mrn, s.first_name || ' ' || s.last_name, s.sex, s.birth_year,
where x.canonical_patient_id = s.patient_id and x.source_system = 'MeadowPoint EHR'
from stg.pm_patient s join patient.patient p on p.patient_id = s.patient_id;

derivations.name: clinical_encounter
sql: select a.appointment_id, a.appointment_id, a.patient_id, a.provider_id, a.clinic_id,
case when a.completed_at is not null then 'completed' else 'open' end,
case when a.completed_at is not null

derivations.name: clinical_diagnosis
derivations._note: Deterministic id-modulo variety (no random() in derivations). Code popularity is banded: the top 15 codes take 45% of principal diagnoses, ranks 2/3 draw from the mid and tail bands so codes rarely repeat within an encounter.

derivations.name: clinical_procedure
derivations._note: Documented procedures = the booked service lines of visits that completed. fk_copy fee x units flows through from scheduling.appointment_service.
sql: select s.service_id, s.appointment_id, s.fee_item_id, s.charge_code, s.service_category,
join scheduling.appointment a on a.appointment_id = s.appointment_id

derivations.name: billing_charge
derivations._note: posted_at follows the billing machine's charges_entered_at with per-line jitter; visits stuck before charge entry stay 'pending' (the DNFB story DQ-001 reports).
sql: select p.procedure_id, p.encounter_id, e.patient_id, cov.payer_id, p.service_date,
case when a.charges_entered_at is not null
case when a.charges_entered_at is not null then 'posted' else 'pending' end,
join clinical.encounter e on e.encounter_id = p.encounter_id
join scheduling.appointment a on a.appointment_id = p.encounter_id
left join patient.coverage cov on cov.patient_id = e.patient_id and cov.coverage_rank = 1;

derivations.name: claims_claim
derivations._note: Claim status maps from the machine's billing_status; denial_reason exists iff the claim was ever denied, with eligibility denials over-represented when registration overrode a failed eligibility check (root-cause coherence).
sql: select a.appointment_id, 'CLM-' || printf('%07d', a.appointment_id), a.appointment_id, a.patient_id,
case when a.denied_at is null then null
join patient.coverage cov on cov.patient_id = a.patient_id and cov.coverage_rank = 1

derivations.name: claims_claim_line
sql: row_number() over (partition by cl.claim_id order by c.charge_id),
join claims.claim cl on cl.encounter_id = c.encounter_id

derivations.name: claims_resubmission
sql: case when a.paid_at is not null then 'paid'

derivations.name: claims_remittance
derivations._note: Disposition identity is exact by construction: contractual = billed - allowed; patient responsibility by payer type (20% coinsurance Medicare, copay-capped commercial, full balance self-pay); paid = allowed - patient responsibility.
sql: select cl.claim_id, cl.payer_id, cl.billed_amount, date(a.paid_at) as remit_date,
join scheduling.appointment a on a.appointment_id = cl.claim_id
join ref.payer py on py.payer_id = cl.payer_id
join patient.coverage cov on cov.coverage_id = cl.coverage_id
select claim_id, '835-' || printf('%07d', claim_id), claim_id, payer_id, remit_date,
case claim_id % 5 when 0 then 'check' else 'EFT' end

derivations.name: workflow_denial_worklist
derivations._note: Open denials only (stuck 'denied' plus exhausted 'denied_final'); case_type 'denial_followup'; queue routes by root cause, assignees are a small fictional biller pool. The mapping-request cases (case_type 'plan_mapping_request') are appended later by workflow_mapping_request once nv_encounter exists.
sql: select cl.claim_id, 'denial_followup', cl.claim_id, 'CLM-' || printf('%07d', cl.claim_id), cl.denial_reason, cl.payer_id, cl.billed_amount,
case cl.claim_id % 6 when 0 then 'D. Acharya' when 1 then 'M. Ferreira'
when 2 then 'K. Boswell' when 3 then 'T. Nakagawa'
when 4 then 'R. Ellison' else 'S. Mwangi' end,
case when cl.claim_status = 'denied_final' and cl.claim_id % 3 = 0 then 'escalated' else 'open' end
join scheduling.appointment a on a.appointment_id = cl.claim_id

derivations.name: wh_dim_date
sql: union all select date(dt, '+1 day') from d where dt < '2025-12-31'
select dt, cast(strftime('%Y', dt) as integer), cast(strftime('%m', dt) as integer),
cast(strftime('%d', dt) as integer), cast(strftime('%w', dt) as integer),
case when strftime('%w', dt) in ('0', '6') then 1 else 0 end

derivations.name: wh_dim_patient
sql: left join ref.clinic cl on cl.clinic_id = c.home_clinic_id;

derivations.name: wh_dim_provider
sql: select p.provider_id, p.provider_name, p.npi, p.credential, p.specialty, c.clinic_name
left join ref.clinic c on c.clinic_id = p.clinic_id;

derivations.name: wh_fact_encounter
sql: select e.encounter_id, e.encounter_date, e.patient_id, e.provider_id, e.clinic_id,
(select count(*) from clinical.procedure p where p.encounter_id = e.encounter_id),
left join patient.coverage cov on cov.patient_id = e.patient_id and cov.coverage_rank = 1;

derivations.name: wh_fact_charge
sql: select c.charge_id, c.service_date, date(c.posted_at), c.patient_id, e.provider_id,
join clinical.encounter e on e.encounter_id = c.encounter_id;

derivations.name: wh_fact_claim
sql: select cl.claim_id, cl.submitted_date, cl.service_date, cl.patient_id, cl.payer_id,
case when r.remit_date is not null
left join claims.remittance r on r.claim_id = cl.claim_id;

derivations.name: raw_ehr_encounter_extract
derivations._note: EHR encounter feed landed verbatim as text with US-format dates; runs after clinical.encounter so the rows exist. ehr_encounter_date_drift corrupts a slice post-derivation (DQ catches via the staged NULLs).
sql: select cast(encounter_id as text), cast(appointment_id as text), cast(patient_id as text),
cast(provider_id as text), cast(clinic_id as text), encounter_type, visit_status,
case when completed_at is not null then strftime('%m/%d/%Y %H:%M:%S', completed_at) else null end,

derivations.name: raw_rcm_charge_extract
derivations._note: Charge interface landed verbatim; runs after billing.charge. service_date drift handled by the case-guarded stg parse.
sql: select cast(charge_id as text), cast(encounter_id as text), cast(patient_id as text),
case when payer_id is null then null else cast(payer_id as text) end,
charge_code, service_category, cast(units as text), printf('%.2f', charge_amount), charge_status,
case when posted_at is not null then strftime('%m/%d/%Y %H:%M:%S', posted_at) else null end,

derivations.name: raw_clearinghouse_837_extract
derivations._note: 837 claim file landed verbatim from the derived claim table. Re-sends are modeled: claims whose claim_id % 37 = 0 land a SECOND time under a later batch_id (BATCH-837B) with a later ingested_at, simulating a rejected-then-resubmitted file. landing_id = claim_id for batch A, claim_id + 100000000 for batch B. stg dedups to the latest batch; DQ-013 reports the superseded first copy. raw_837_date_drift corrupts service dates post-derivation (DQ-012).
sql: select claim_id, cast(claim_id as text), claim_number, cast(encounter_id as text), cast(patient_id as text),
cast(payer_id as text), printf('%.2f', billed_amount), claim_status,
case when submitted_date is not null then strftime('%m/%d/%Y', submitted_date) else null end,
select claim_id + 100000000, cast(claim_id as text), claim_number, cast(encounter_id as text), cast(patient_id as text),
cast(payer_id as text), printf('%.2f', billed_amount), claim_status,
case when submitted_date is not null then strftime('%m/%d/%Y', submitted_date) else null end,

derivations.name: raw_remit_835_extract
derivations._note: 835 remittance file landed verbatim from the derived remittance table; runs after claims.remittance.
sql: select cast(remittance_id as text), remit_number, cast(claim_id as text), cast(payer_id as text),
printf('%.2f', billed_amount), printf('%.2f', allowed_amount), printf('%.2f', contractual_adjustment),

derivations.name: stg_ehr_encounter
derivations._note: Typed/trimmed; case-guarded date parse (drifted formats land NULL).
sql: select cast(encounter_id as integer), cast(appointment_id as integer), cast(patient_id as integer),
cast(provider_id as integer), cast(clinic_id as integer), lower(encounter_type), lower(visit_status),

derivations.name: stg_rcm_charge
sql: select cast(charge_id as integer), cast(encounter_id as integer), cast(patient_id as integer),
case when payer_id is null then null else cast(payer_id as integer) end,
trim(charge_code), lower(service_category), cast(units as integer), cast(charge_amount as real), lower(charge_status),

derivations.name: stg_claim_837
derivations._note: Dedup of re-sent clearinghouse files: row_number partition by claim_id ordered by ingested_at desc keeps the latest batch only. The superseded first copy is what DQ-013 reports.
sql: select claim_id, claim_number, encounter_id, patient_id, payer_id, billed_amount, claim_status, service_date, submitted_date, source_batch_id, ingested_at
select cast(claim_id as integer) as claim_id, trim(claim_number) as claim_number,
cast(encounter_id as integer) as encounter_id, cast(patient_id as integer) as patient_id,
cast(payer_id as integer) as payer_id, cast(billed_amount as real) as billed_amount,
row_number() over (partition by cast(claim_id as integer) order by ingested_at desc, batch_id desc) as rn

derivations.name: stg_remit_835
sql: select cast(remittance_id as integer), trim(remit_number), cast(claim_id as integer), cast(payer_id as integer),
cast(billed_amount as real), cast(allowed_amount as real), cast(contractual_adjustment as real),
cast(patient_responsibility as real), cast(paid_amount as real),

derivations.name: nv_encounter
derivations._note: Normalized view (rung 6): the central wide re-join of the encounter fact to its patient/provider/payer/date dims with business column names. No aggregation. left joins because EMPI-unmastered patients and missing-coverage encounters legitimately lose dim rows. BOTH competing BU views build on this view. payer_key/plan_code carried as degenerate keys; payer name resolved from ref.payer (small dim, fully covered). billable_flag is the documented exclusion: a completed encounter with posted charges > 0.
sql: case when f.visit_status = 'completed' and f.gross_charges > 0 then 1 else 0 end as billable_flag
left join wh.dim_patient dp on dp.patient_key = f.patient_key
left join wh.dim_provider dpr on dpr.provider_key = f.provider_key
left join ref.clinic cl on cl.clinic_id = f.clinic_id
left join ref.payer py on py.payer_id = f.payer_key
left join wh.dim_date dd on dd.date_key = f.encounter_date;

derivations.name: workflow_mapping_request
derivations._note: Mapping-request cases: one per distinct billable plan code with no approved active financial-class mapping (the same UNMAPPED population mart_revcycle drops to UNMAPPED and DQ-011 reports). case_id offset by 9,000,000 so it never collides with claim-derived case_ids. Routed to the revenue-cycle mapping queue.
sql: select 9000000 + row_number() over (order by n.plan_code), 'plan_mapping_request', null, n.plan_code, null, null, null,
from (select distinct plan_code from nv_encounter where billable_flag = 1 and plan_code is not null) n
where not exists (select 1 from manual.payer_plan_grouping m

derivations.name: nv_charge
derivations._note: Normalized charge-line view over fact_charge re-joined to patient/payer dims. No aggregation.
sql: select f.charge_id, f.service_date, f.posted_date, f.patient_key, dp.mrn,
left join wh.dim_patient dp on dp.patient_key = f.patient_key
left join wh.dim_provider dpr on dpr.provider_key = f.provider_key
left join ref.clinic cl on cl.clinic_id = f.clinic_id
left join ref.payer py on py.payer_id = f.payer_key;

derivations.name: nv_claim
derivations._note: Normalized claim view over fact_claim re-joined to patient/payer dims and the staged 837 (the deduped clearinghouse copy). No aggregation.
sql: select f.claim_id, f.submitted_date, f.service_date, f.patient_key, dp.mrn,
left join wh.dim_patient dp on dp.patient_key = f.patient_key
left join ref.payer py on py.payer_id = f.payer_key
left join stg.claim_837 s on s.claim_id = f.claim_id;

derivations.name: bv_revenue_cycle_funnel
derivations._note: Business view (rung 7): charge -> claim -> remit funnel by claim with lag windows. lag() over service-date order computes the gap to the prior claim's submission for trend context; the charge/claim/remit lag-day columns measure the revenue-cycle latency at each hop. Built on nv_claim joined to staged charge and remit.
sql: select c.claim_id, c.service_date, c.submitted_date, c.payer_name, c.payer_type, c.billed_amount, c.claim_status,
(select min(rc.posted_date) from stg.rcm_charge rc where rc.encounter_id = c.claim_id and rc.charge_status = 'posted') as first_charge_posted,
left join stg.remit_835 r on r.claim_id = c.claim_id
select claim_id, service_date, submitted_date, payer_name, payer_type, billed_amount, claim_status,
case when first_charge_posted is not null and submitted_date is not null
then cast(julianday(submitted_date) - julianday(first_charge_posted) as integer) end as charge_to_submit_days,
case when remit_date is not null and submitted_date is not null
then cast(julianday(remit_date) - julianday(submitted_date) as integer) end as submit_to_remit_days,
lag(submitted_date) over (partition by payer_name order by submitted_date, claim_id)) as integer) as days_since_prior_payer_claim

derivations.name: bv_ar_by_payer
derivations._note: Business view: open accounts-receivable aging case ladder by payer over nv_claim. Open AR = claims submitted but not yet paid/written off. Aging buckets via a case ladder on days since submission as of the as_of date.
sql: round(sum(case when cast(julianday('2025-12-31') - julianday(submitted_date) as integer) <= 30 then billed_amount else 0 end), 2) as ar_0_30,
round(sum(case when cast(julianday('2025-12-31') - julianday(submitted_date) as integer) between 31 and 60 then billed_amount else 0 end), 2) as ar_31_60,
round(sum(case when cast(julianday('2025-12-31') - julianday(submitted_date) as integer) between 61 and 90 then billed_amount else 0 end), 2) as ar_61_90,
round(sum(case when cast(julianday('2025-12-31') - julianday(submitted_date) as integer) > 90 then billed_amount else 0 end), 2) as ar_over_90
where claim_status not in ('paid', 'written_off') and submitted_date is not null

derivations.name: bv_provider_productivity
derivations._note: Business view: window-ranked provider productivity over nv_encounter. Completed encounters and RVUs per provider, with rank() and ntile() tiers. RVUs come from the chargemaster via the procedure lines on completed encounters.
sql: join ref.fee_schedule fs on fs.fee_item_id = pr.fee_item_id
join wh.fact_encounter fe on fe.encounter_id = pr.encounter_id
select provider_key, provider_name, specialty, completed_encounters, gross_charges, total_rvu,
rank() over (order by completed_encounters desc) as encounter_rank,
ntile(4) over (order by total_rvu desc) as rvu_quartile

derivations.name: mv_month_end_ar_snapshot
derivations._note: Materialized view (rung 8): insert-select from bv_ar_by_payer rolled to month grain over the claim submission month. The refresh_mv_month_end_ar_snapshot code object refreshes it on a schedule. ~24 months, scale_exempt.
sql: round(sum(case when cast(julianday('2025-12-31') - julianday(submitted_date) as integer) <= 30 then billed_amount else 0 end), 2) as ar_0_30,
round(sum(case when cast(julianday('2025-12-31') - julianday(submitted_date) as integer) between 31 and 60 then billed_amount else 0 end), 2) as ar_31_60,
round(sum(case when cast(julianday('2025-12-31') - julianday(submitted_date) as integer) between 61 and 90 then billed_amount else 0 end), 2) as ar_61_90,
round(sum(case when cast(julianday('2025-12-31') - julianday(submitted_date) as integer) > 90 then billed_amount else 0 end), 2) as ar_over_90
where claim_status not in ('paid', 'written_off') and submitted_date is not null

derivations.name: mart_clinicalops_encounter_volume
derivations._note: Business-unit view (rung 9), CLINICAL OPS. Counts ALL completed encounters with the RAW plan code (no financial-class mapping, no billable exclusion). Competes with mart_revcycle_encounter_volume on encounter volume.

derivations.name: mart_revcycle_encounter_volume
derivations._note: Business-unit view (rung 9), REVENUE CYCLE. Counts BILLABLE encounters only (billable_flag = 1: completed AND gross_charges > 0, i.e. excludes zero-charge / no-show-converted completed visits) AND applies the human-entered payer_plan_grouping (left join + coalesce to UNMAPPED), deduping the conflicting MCR-ADV-PPO re-upload to the latest approved active row via row_number. TOTAL-PRESERVING grouping: every billable encounter counts once under its financial class or UNMAPPED.
sql: row_number() over (partition by source_code order by uploaded_at desc, mapping_id desc) as rn
where approved_flag = 1 and (effective_end_date is null or effective_end_date > '2025-12-31')
left join mapping m on m.source_code = n.plan_code and m.rn = 1

derivations.name: control_recon_revcycle_clinicalops
derivations._note: Reconciles the competing encounter-volume metric (complements control_recon_charge_claim). clinicalops counts all completed encounters; revcycle counts billable only. difference = clinicalops - revcycle; excluded_count = completed non-billable (zero-charge) encounters; unexplained_difference = difference - excluded_count MUST be 0 (the mapping is total-preserving, so the only legitimate gap is the documented billable exclusion). revcycle_unmapped is the count of billable encounters that fell to the UNMAPPED financial class.
sql: with co as (select count(*) as clinicalops_encounters from nv_encounter where visit_status = 'completed'),
rc as (select count(*) as revcycle_encounters from nv_encounter where billable_flag = 1),
ex as (select count(*) as excluded_count from nv_encounter where visit_status = 'completed' and billable_flag = 0),
and not exists (select 1 from manual.payer_plan_grouping m

derivations.name: mart_clinic_volume_daily
derivations._note: Mart as a VIEW so it reflects post-imperfection data. Shows the weekday-clinic shape and the ~12% no-show rate by site.
sql: sum(case when a.status = 'completed' then 1 else 0 end) as visits_completed,
sum(case when a.status = 'no_show' then 1 else 0 end) as no_shows,
sum(case when a.status = 'cancelled' then 1 else 0 end) as cancellations,
round(1.0 * sum(case when a.status = 'no_show' then 1 else 0 end) /
nullif(count(*) - sum(case when a.status = 'cancelled' then 1 else 0 end), 0), 3) as no_show_rate
join ref.clinic c on c.clinic_id = a.clinic_id

derivations.name: mart_revenue_cycle_monthly
derivations._note: Rewired onto the stack: BU view over nv_claim (the normalized claim view), joined to staged remittances for cash and days-to-pay.
sql: sum(case when cl.denial_reason is not null then 1 else 0 end) as claims_ever_denied,
round(1.0 * sum(case when cl.denial_reason is not null then 1 else 0 end) /
nullif(sum(case when cl.claim_status in ('paid','denied','denied_final','written_off','resubmitted','appealed') then 1 else 0 end), 0), 3) as denial_rate,
round(avg(case when r.remit_date is not null
then julianday(r.remit_date) - julianday(cl.submitted_date) end), 1) as avg_days_to_pay
left join stg.remit_835 r on r.claim_id = cl.claim_id

derivations.name: control_recon_charge_claim
derivations._note: Reconciliation as a VIEW: claim lines are a snapshot at claim generation, while billing.charge keeps moving (post-load restatement pairs and interface drops) - exactly how real charge-to-claim recons break.
sql: select cl.claim_id, cl.encounter_id, cl.billed_amount as claim_billed,
round(coalesce(sum(case when c.charge_status = 'posted' then c.charge_amount end), 0), 2) as charges_posted_now,
round(cl.billed_amount - coalesce(sum(case when c.charge_status = 'posted' then c.charge_amount end), 0), 2) as break_amount
left join billing.charge c on c.encounter_id = cl.encounter_id
having abs(cl.billed_amount - round(coalesce(sum(case when c.charge_status = 'posted' then c.charge_amount end), 0), 2)) > 0.01;

derivations.name: dq_rule_result_current
sql: select 'DQ-001' as rule_code, 'completed encounter with no posted charges' as issue, cast(e.encounter_id as text) as entity_id
where e.visit_status = 'completed' and e.encounter_date <= date('2025-12-31', '-10 days')
and not exists (select 1 from billing.charge c where c.encounter_id = e.encounter_id and c.charge_status = 'posted')
select 'DQ-002', 'procedure references missing encounter', cast(p.procedure_id as text)
where not exists (select 1 from clinical.encounter e where e.encounter_id = p.encounter_id)
select 'DQ-003', 'patient missing source identifier mapping', cast(p.patient_id as text)
where (select count(distinct x.source_system) from xref.patient_identifier x
from raw.pm_patient_extract r where r.registration_date not like '__/__/____'
select 'DQ-005', 'claim service date after coverage termination', cast(cl.claim_id as text)
from claims.claim cl join patient.coverage cov on cov.coverage_id = cl.coverage_id
where cov.term_date is not null and cl.service_date > cov.term_date
select 'DQ-006', 'staging last name disagrees with PM source', cast(s.patient_id as text)
from stg.pm_patient s join patient.patient p on p.patient_id = s.patient_id
select 'DQ-007', 'duplicate interface job delivery', cast(jr.run_id as text)
where j2.job_name = jr.job_name and j2.run_started_at = jr.run_started_at and j2.run_id <> jr.run_id)
select 'DQ-008', 'charge posted more than 5 days after service', cast(c.charge_id as text)
where c.posted_at is not null and julianday(c.posted_at) - julianday(c.service_date) > 5
select 'DQ-009', 'possible duplicate patient registration', cast(p.patient_id as text)
where q.first_name = p.first_name and q.birth_year = p.birth_year
and q.city = p.city and q.patient_id <> p.patient_id)
select 'DQ-010', 'past appointment never reconciled from scheduled', cast(a.appointment_id as text)
where a.status = 'scheduled' and a.appointment_date <= date('2025-12-31', '-7 days')
select 'DQ-011', 'billable plan code missing approved financial-class mapping', n.plan_code
from (select distinct plan_code from nv_encounter where billable_flag = 1 and plan_code is not null) n
where not exists (select 1 from manual.payer_plan_grouping m
select 'DQ-012', 'unparseable raw 837 claim service date', claim_id
from raw.clearinghouse_837_extract where service_date_raw not like '__/__/____'
select 'DQ-013', 're-sent 837 claim file superseded by later batch', r.claim_id || '|' || r.batch_id
where r2.claim_id = r.claim_id and r2.ingested_at > r.ingested_at);

derivations.name: catalog_lineage_edge
derivations._note: Lineage edges for the authored procedures/function/extracts -> the tables they read and write.
sql: insert into catalog.lineage_edge (edge_id, code_object, operation, table_name, edge_note) values

derivations.name: catalog_code_object
derivations._note: Authored target-platform source for the procedures/function/extracts, then self-registration of every deployed view from sqlite_master so the catalog provably matches the code. Runs LAST so all views exist. age_at_service is intentionally stale (references a retired column) - noted as known debt.
sql: insert into catalog.code_object (object_name, object_type, language, source_text, owner, created_at, last_altered_at, notes) values

('nightly_claim_scrub', 'procedure', 'plpgsql', 'create or replace procedure nightly_claim_scrub() language plpgsql as $$ begin update claims_claim set claim_status = ''scrub_hold'' where claim_status = ''submitted'' and exists (select 1 from patient_coverage cov where cov.patient_id = claims_claim.patient_id and cov.coverage_rank = 1 and cov.verified_flag = 0); end; $$;', 'revenue_integrity', '2024-01-18 09:00:00', '2025-03-04 14:10:00', 'Scheduled nightly by integration.job nightly_claim_scrub; holds claims failing eligibility scrub.'),

('month_end_charge_close', 'procedure', 'plpgsql', 'create or replace procedure month_end_charge_close(as_of date) language plpgsql as $$ begin insert into workflow_denial_worklist (case_type, queue, case_status, opened_date) select ''dnfb_followup'', ''billing_follow_up'', ''open'', as_of from clinical_encounter e where e.visit_status = ''completed'' and not exists (select 1 from billing_charge c where c.encounter_id = e.encounter_id and c.charge_status = ''posted'') and age_at_service(e.patient_id, e.encounter_date) >= 0; end; $$;', 'revenue_integrity', '2024-01-25 08:30:00', '2025-01-31 18:05:00', 'Month-end DNFB sweep; opens follow-up cases for completed visits with no posted charges; calls age_at_service.'),

('refresh_mv_month_end_ar_snapshot', 'procedure', 'sql', 'create or replace procedure refresh_mv_month_end_ar_snapshot() language sql as $$ delete from mv_month_end_ar_snapshot; insert into mv_month_end_ar_snapshot (month_key, open_claims, ar_billed, ar_0_30, ar_31_60, ar_61_90, ar_over_90) select strftime(''%Y-%m'', submitted_date), count(*), round(sum(billed_amount),2), 0, 0, 0, 0 from nv_claim where claim_status not in (''paid'',''written_off'') and submitted_date is not null group by 1; $$;', 'data_engineering', '2024-02-02 09:00:00', '2025-02-03 11:20:00', 'Monthly matview rebuild scheduled by integration.job refresh_mv_month_end_ar_snapshot.'),

('age_at_service', 'function', 'sql', 'create or replace function age_at_service(p_patient_id integer, p_service_date date) returns integer language sql as $$ select cast(strftime(''%Y'', p_service_date) as integer) - p.date_of_birth_year from patient_patient p where p.patient_id = p_patient_id; $$;', 'data_engineering', '2023-10-12 10:00:00', '2023-10-12 10:00:00', 'KNOWN DEBT: references retired column patient.date_of_birth_year (renamed to birth_year in the 2024 PM upgrade); flagged for cleanup.'),

('outbound_837_submission', 'extract', 'sql', 'select c.claim_number, c.service_date, c.payer_id, c.billed_amount, l.charge_code, l.units, l.billed_amount as line_amount from claims_claim c join claims_claim_line l on l.claim_id = c.claim_id where c.claim_status in (''submitted'',''in_adjudication'') order by c.claim_number, l.line_number; -- format: X12 837P; destination: sftp://atlas-clearing.example.com/out/; schedule: 0 23 * * *', 'integration_eng', '2024-03-05 09:00:00', '2025-04-12 09:15:00', 'Outbound professional-claim feed to the clearinghouse (job outbound_837_submission).'),

('state_immunization_registry_extract', 'extract', 'sql', 'select pr.service_date, pr.charge_code, pr.units, e.patient_id, pr.performed_by from clinical_procedure pr join clinical_encounter e on e.encounter_id = pr.encounter_id where pr.service_category = ''immunization''; -- format: HL7 VXU; destination: sftp://state-iis.example.gov/in/; schedule: 0 1 * * 1', 'integration_eng', '2024-04-01 09:00:00', '2025-05-02 10:00:00', 'Weekly state immunization registry submission (job state_immunization_registry_extract).');
insert into catalog.code_object (object_name, object_type, language, source_text, owner, created_at, last_altered_at, notes)
from sqlite_master where type = 'view' and name not like 'sqlite_%';

imperfections.name: patient_name_typos
imperfections.scenario: Hand-keyed registrations accumulate name typos; they propagate through the extract into staging and the EMPI.

imperfections.name: duplicate_patient_registrations
imperfections.scenario: Front desk re-registers patients instead of searching; DQ-009 surfaces the merge candidates for the EMPI queue.

imperfections.name: coverage_stale_terms
imperfections.scenario: Coverage termed by the employer but still on file at registration; claims billed against it trip DQ-005 (eligibility denials story).

imperfections.name: registration_eligibility_overrides
imperfections.scenario: Registration bypasses failed eligibility checks; the denial worklist carries the flag and those visits skew toward eligibility denials.

imperfections.name: duplicate_job_runs
imperfections.scenario: Scheduler retries log duplicate interface runs; DQ-007 dedups them.

imperfections.name: raw_extract_date_drift
imperfections.scenario: Legacy extract job pads dates after a patch; DQ-004 catches the unparseable slice.

imperfections.name: stg_name_conflicts
imperfections.scenario: EHR-sourced demographic merge mangles some staged names vs the PM source; DQ-006 reports the disagreement.

imperfections.name: empi_xref_gaps
imperfections.scenario: Mappings lost during the 2024 EMPI migration; DQ-003 counts patients with incomplete crosswalks.

imperfections.name: orphan_procedures
imperfections.scenario: An EHR encounter purge corrupted procedure links; DQ-002 is the orphan-procedure queue.

imperfections.name: charge_feed_drops
imperfections.scenario: Charge interface drops lines after claims were generated - encounters lose charges (DQ-001) and claim-vs-charge recon breaks (CTL-001).

imperfections.name: charge_restatements
imperfections.scenario: Charge corrections issued as reversal + restated pairs after claim generation; these create the CTL-001 recon breaks.

imperfections.name: late_charge_posts
imperfections.scenario: Late charge entry beyond the 5-day standard; DQ-008 tracks the late-charge rate.

imperfections.name: raw_837_date_format_drift
imperfections.scenario: Clearinghouse 837 service-date format drifts after an edit-rule version bump; DQ-012 catches the unparseable slice and the stg parse lands NULL.

imperfections.name: raw_encounter_date_format_drift
imperfections.scenario: EHR encounter extract pads dates differently after a patch; the case-guarded stg parse lands those NULL.

controls.rule_name: Claim billed ties to posted charges

controls.expected_break_rate: 0 (identity by construction: paid + contractual + patient = billed)

controls.rule_name: EMPI patients tie to source mappings
Revenue-cycle billable encounters reconcile to clinical-ops completed encounters
controls.expected_break_rate: difference equals the non-billable (zero-charge) completed-encounter count; unexplained_difference must be 0

dq_rules.condition: completed encounters have posted charges within 10 days
every patient mapped in both PM and EHR
claim service date within coverage window
staged last name matches PM source
one delivery per job per start time
charges post within 5 days of service
no duplicate first name + birth year + city registrations
every billable plan code has an approved financial-class mapping
one effective 837 copy per claim (latest batch supersedes)

dataflows.name: patient_mastering
dataflows.sequence: patient.patient -> raw.pm_patient_extract -> stg.pm_patient -> xref.patient_identifier -> core.patient -> wh.dim_patient

dataflows.name: visit_to_cash
dataflows.sequence: scheduling.appointment (+services) -> scheduling machine -> clinical.encounter/diagnosis/procedure -> billing.charge -> claims.claim/claim_line -> claim machine -> claims.remittance -> wh facts -> marts

dataflows.name: claim_837_landing
dataflows.sequence: claims.claim -> raw.clearinghouse_837_extract (with BATCH-837B re-sends) -> stg.claim_837 (row_number dedup) -> DQ-013

dataflows.name: view_stack
dataflows.sequence: wh facts/dims -> nv_encounter/nv_charge/nv_claim -> bv_revenue_cycle_funnel/bv_ar_by_payer/bv_provider_productivity -> mv_month_end_ar_snapshot / mart_revcycle_encounter_volume / mart_clinicalops_encounter_volume / mart_revenue_cycle_monthly

dataflows.name: denial_management
dataflows.sequence: claim machine (denied) -> claims.claim.denial_reason -> workflow.denial_worklist (denial_followup) -> claims.resubmission / appeal outcomes

dataflows.name: plan_grouping
dataflows.sequence: manual.payer_plan_grouping -> mart_revcycle_encounter_volume (applied) vs mart_clinicalops_encounter_volume (not) -> control_recon_revcycle_clinicalops + DQ-011 + workflow.denial_worklist (plan_mapping_request)

dataflows.name: code_catalog
dataflows.sequence: sqlite_master views + authored procs/function/extracts -> catalog.code_object; catalog.lineage_edge; integration.job -> integration.job_run
